originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and bicycle scripts, then initializes many inline rider, force, wheel, stability, and frame sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, route-local model assets, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, the local bicycle model assets, and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the drag, play-pause, force, slip, and later torsion scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a rider-controlled bicycle view, then steps through crate-force examples with slider-driven motion, restart controls, and synchronized vector and speed readouts.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening rider scene, dragable viewpoint, early force sliders, and restartable motion demonstrations under the original published runtime.
notes: The smoke contract treats one opening drag path, one play-pause path, and one early force-slider path as required parity behavior.
The route preserves the published progression from rider overview into Newtonian force examples instead of flattening those sections into static diagrams.
evidence: Post-earth-and-sun rollout planning explicitly called for one rider or force slider, one play-pause or restart path, and responsive-shell validation.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening hero scene responds to drag, the published play-pause control toggles state, and an early force scene updates from slider input.

originalBehavior: The published article continues through longitudinal, lateral, slip-angle, cornering, and stability sections with linked bicycle geometry, wheel contact, and slider-driven state changes.
localStatus: The local route keeps the wheel, slip, and stability scenes intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The published 3D bicycle scenes now load their model data from the route-local model tree instead of the upstream root path.
The shipped `gears` reference is rewritten locally while not-yet-shipped Bartosz article links remain neutralized in the public body.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one wheel or slip scene as parity-critical for the bicycle batch.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a slip-angle scene updates from slider input without runtime asset failures.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with wheel-structure, frame-stress, bending, torsion, and FEM sections, followed by an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance.
localStatus: The local route keeps the later mechanical sections through the original scripts while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The public body no longer includes the Patreon footer prompt or other author-promo surfaces.
evidence: The hardened Ciechanowski audit flags ordinary in-body upstream article links while allowing footer-only provenance.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a later torsion scene remains interactive and the route stays responsive without upstream asset dependencies.
